mnemonics: Queen's University is Excellent Everywhere Using Stacks and Queues
Queens Never Eat Unusual Sandwiches And Wake Up Early
keyPoints: A queue is a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) data structure which means the first element added to the queue will be removed first.
A circular queue is similar to a regular queue, but it has a circular buffer which means the last element added to the queue will be removed first.
You can implement a queue using a dynamic array by using two pointers, one at the front of the queue and one at the rear of the queue.
You can implement multiple queues using multiple stacks and queues, where each stack and queue can be used independently.
A stack is a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) data structure, whereas a queue is a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) data structure.
